purpose: Tenth AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering recipient-side init-family command-message consumption from a pulled upstream token.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel carrying the upstream wire-r-init command-message token.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel, empty during this recipient init trace.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel, empty during this recipient init trace.

layers.summary: A wire-r-init command-message token reconfigures the recipient into a wire with right memory.
layers.boundary: This layer states recipient init command-message consumption only; it does not execute standard-signal, write-buffer, or multi-command inputs.

layers.summary: The recipient init node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete multi-cell command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one recipient init command-message activation.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0049/ADR-0050 init-family recipient subset and adds no non-init command semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested recipient init command-message transition.
